steps.title: Identify the heliograph’s stated limitations
steps.content:
The passage lists two constraints: heliographs worked only during the daytime, and their range depended on opacity of the air and line of sight. The word “Therefore” signals that the successor technology overcame exactly these problems. Eliminate choices that don’t address daytime-only use and environmental dependence.
  • Choice A — ‘worked on similar principles but was easier to produce and maintain.’: Ease of production and maintenance has nothing to do with the daytime or visibility constraints described. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘was not so constrained by environmental circumstances.’: This directly addresses both limitations — daytime-only use and dependence on air opacity and line of sight are all environmental constraints. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘could be used for more than military, surveying, or forestry purposes.’: Expanding the range of applications doesn’t resolve the daytime or visibility problems. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘enabled communication that didn't require knowledge of Morse code.’: The passage mentions Morse code only as the form of the signal, not as a limitation driving replacement. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content:
Only the credited option resolves the stated constraints: the replacement technology was not limited by time of day or atmospheric conditions.

That confirms B is correct.
